title: 3 Dividend Stocks Defying the Market Downturn Amid the Iran Conflict
author: Nathan Reiff
description: While the S&P 500 has fallen nearly 5% since the start of the U.S.-Iran conflict, three dividend-paying stocks are bucking the trend: Crescent Energy, Viper Energy Partners, and Unum Group. Crescent Energy benefits from its Permian Basin operations and recent analyst upgrades. Viper Energy's royalty model shields it from operational risks while offering a 3.3% dividend yield. Unum Group, a life and disability insurer, stands out as a non-energy play with expected 8%-12% EPS growth and 30% upside potential.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Recent analyst upgrades from JPMorgan Chase, Wells Fargo, and Piper Sandler; strong production growth (268,000 BOE/d); $239 million in levered free cash flow; advantageous domestic shale positioning benefits from Middle East supply concerns; 2.5% dividend yield with expanding cash flow

Multiple analyst price target increases with consensus target 15% above current levels; $8 billion in mineral acquisitions in 2025; improved balance sheet; royalty model provides downside protection from operational risks; 3.3% dividend yield with new share repurchase program

Expected 8%-12% EPS growth and 4%-7% core operations growth for 2026; sustained profitability with nearly two decades of consistent dividend increases; 2.49% dividend yield; approximately 30% upside potential; benefits from market dislocation despite being fundamentally disconnected from Iran conflict

Expected 8%-12% EPS growth and 4%-7% core operations growth for 2026; sustained profitability with nearly two decades of consistent dividend increases; 2.49% dividend yield; approximately 30% upside potential; benefits from market dislocation despite being fundamentally disconnected from Iran conflict
Up more than 3% in the past month due to higher oil prices resulting from Iran conflict; benefits energy sector broadly

title: Goldman Sachs Raises Oil Price Forecasts – Picks 7 Winners Among Energy Stocks
author: Piero Cingari
description: Goldman Sachs upgraded its oil price forecasts following the Strait of Hormuz closure, expecting Brent crude to average $80-$100/barrel in 2026 and $75/barrel in 2027. The bank identified seven energy stocks as winners, with Permian-focused E&Ps and major oil companies positioned to benefit from higher oil prices and strong cash flow generation through major projects.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Highest-conviction buy on Goldman's US Conviction List with projected 20-25% CAGR in free cash flow per share through 2030, driven by four major projects and $1B in cost reductions. 16% upside potential.
Leading Canadian oil name with 25% estimated total return as West White Rose project approaches first oil by end of Q2 2026.
Permian-focused E&P with raised price target implying 13% upside. Offers 12% average FCF yield on 2027/2028 estimates versus 10% large-cap peer average.
Permian E&P with $66 price target and 16% upside potential. Pending $3.0B divestiture of Anadarko assets expected to further reduce net debt.
Stands out on yield with 16% average FCF yield on 2027/2028 estimates at $75/bbl Brent versus 11% E&P peer average. 15% upside potential.
Largest potential percentage gain at 29% upside with 37% total return. No-capex mineral royalty model compresses cost structure and amplifies margins.
Major oil company with $217 price target and 10% estimated total return, supported by $28-30B annual cash flow guidance by decade's end and $15B share repurchases through 2028.

title: This Energy Stock Returned $131 Million to Shareholders Last Quarter, but One Fund Still Sold Off $48 Million in Shares
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Webs Creek Capital Management sold 1.27 million shares of Viper Energy (worth ~$48.2 million) in Q4 2025, reducing its stake to 1.87% of AUM. Despite the fund's reduction, Viper Energy returned $131 million to shareholders last quarter through dividends and buybacks, with a 5% dividend yield and recent 15% base dividend increase. The stock has underperformed the S&P 500 over the past year despite strong operational metrics.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong shareholder returns ($131M returned last quarter), increased dividend (15% raise), expanded buyback authorization ($1B), robust cash generation ($145M available for distribution), and growing production (134,000 boe/day). Despite institutional selling and stock underperformance, fundamentals remain attractive with a 5% dividend yield.
Mentioned as parent company of Viper Energy, providing scale and resources. No specific news or performance data provided about Diamondback itself in the article.

title: Viper Energy Launches Secondary Common Stock Offering By Diamondback Energy, Inc. and Certain Affiliates of EnCap Investments, L.P. and Oaktree Capital Management, L.P.
author: Na
description: Viper Energy announced an underwritten public offering of 17.4 million shares of Class A common stock by Diamondback Energy and affiliates of EnCap Investments and Oaktree Capital Management. Viper will not receive proceeds from the sale but has agreed to purchase 1 million units in its operating company from Oaktree affiliates at the offering price. J.P. Morgan and Goldman Sachs are joint book-running managers.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The secondary offering is a capital structure transaction that does not directly impact Viper's operations or financial performance. While the concurrent OpCo unit purchase shows confidence from management, the offering itself is a selling event by existing shareholders with no proceeds to the company.

As the parent company and primary selling shareholder, Diamondback is divesting shares in Viper. This is a liquidity event for shareholders but does not indicate operational changes or strategic concerns about the subsidiary.

title: Guardian Wealth Takes $5.1 Million Stake in Viper Energy, According to Recent SEC Filing
author: Jake Lerch
description: Guardian Wealth Management invested $5.1 million in Viper Energy during Q3 2025, acquiring 133,874 shares, representing 3.02% of their assets under management. Viper Energy is a royalty-based oil and gas company with strong historical performance.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong historical stock performance (298% over 10 years), high dividend yield (5.8%), and stable royalty-based business model
